zh.of Mirfield, Yorks., Eng: 英國英格蘭越可爾米費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Thomas Foxe於1521年出生在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾米費爾德時，他的父親Fox22歲，母親Jennet Gerard22歲。他於1542年與Margaret Purrett在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾米費爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1575年5月在英國英格蘭越可爾米費爾德享年54歲，埋葬在英國越可爾米費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Foxe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Fox
meanings: English: nickname from a word denoting the animal (Middle English, Old English fox), widely used to denote a sly or cunning individual. It was also used for someone with red hair. In England this surname absorbed some early examples of surnames derived from the ancient Germanic personal names mentioned at Faulks and Foulks .
Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an tSionnaigh ‘son of the fox’ (see Tinney ).
Irish: also adopted for Ó Catharnaigh, see Kearney .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Thomas Foxe was born in 1521, in Mirfield, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Fox, was 22 and his mother, Jennet Gerard, was 22. He married Margaret Purrett in 1542, in Mirfield, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He died in May 1575, in Mirfield, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54, and was buried in Mirfield, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Thomas Foxe est né en 1521, à Mirfield, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Fox, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Jennet Gerard, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Purrett en 1542, à Mirfield, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en mai 1575, à Mirfield, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Mirfield, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Thomas Foxeが1521年にイギリスイギリスウエスト ヨークシュアイアミルフィールドで生まれた時，父Foxは22歳，母Jennet Gerardは22歳であった。1542年にイギリスイギリスウエスト ヨークシュアイアミルフィールドでMargaret Purrettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1575年5月にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアミルフィールドで54歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアミルフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Thomas Foxe(이)가 1521년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 미르필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fox(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Jennet Gerard(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1542년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 미르필드에서 Margaret Purrett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1575년 5월 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 미르필드에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 미르필드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Thomas Foxe родился в 1521 г. в местности мирфиелд, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Fox было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Jennet Gerard, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Purrett в 1542 г. в местности мирфиелд, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Он умер май 1575 г. в местности мирфиелд, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности мирфиелд, еркшир, Англия.
